quarantine.oracle: The alignment semantics (reference positions, bases aligned) are preserved after normalizing CIGAR match operators (M, =, X) to their canonical form

evidence.quote: CIGAR operators M, =, and X all represent alignment matches; M is the original generic match while = and X provide base-level precision. Normalizing between them preserves alignment semantics.

quarantine.oracle: The mate reference name semantics are preserved after normalizing the '=' alias in RNEXT to the explicit reference name from the header

evidence.quote: In SAM format, RNEXT field may use '=' to denote the same reference as RNAME; canonicalizing this to the explicit reference name preserves the mate mapping information.

quarantine.oracle: The alignment representation is semantically equivalent after removing zero-length CIGAR operations, as they contribute no alignment information

evidence.quote: Zero-length CIGAR operations have no effect on the alignment representation and can be removed without changing the alignment semantics.

quarantine.oracle: The biological sequence information is preserved after normalizing the case of sequence bases (upper/lower case normalization)

evidence.quote: SAM SEQ field may contain upper or lower case bases; case is not semantically meaningful for alignment, only the base identity matters.

quarantine.oracle: The unmapped read semantics are preserved after normalizing fields (RNAME, POS, MAPQ, CIGAR, RNEXT, PNEXT, TLEN) according to SAM specification rules for unmapped reads

evidence.quote: For unmapped reads, SAM specification defines canonical values for RNAME (*), POS (0), MAPQ (255), CIGAR (*), etc. Normalizing these fields preserves the unmapped status semantics.

quarantine.oracle: The record semantics are preserved after permuting the order of optional tag fields, as tags are identified by their two-letter key, not their position

evidence.quote: SAM optional tags are identified by their unique two-letter tag keys; the order of tags in a record is not semantically meaningful.

quarantine.oracle: The header semantics are preserved after reordering header record types (HD, SQ, RG, PG, etc.) according to the canonical order specified in the SAM format

evidence.quote: SAM header records have a conventional ordering (HD first, then SQ, then RG, then PG, then others) but the specification allows flexibility; canonical reordering preserves all information.

quarantine.oracle: The header semantics are preserved after bumping the minor version number in the HD VN tag, as minor version bumps indicate backward-compatible changes

evidence.quote: The SAM format uses semantic versioning where minor version bumps indicate backward-compatible additions; normalizing to a newer minor version preserves all semantics.

quarantine.oracle: The header semantics are preserved after shuffling the order of subtags within header record types (HD, SQ, RG, PG), as subtags are identified by their two-letter keys, not their order

evidence.quote: Header record subtags are identified by their two-letter keys; the order of subtags within a header record is not semantically meaningful.

quarantine.oracle: The header semantics are preserved after shuffling the order of CO (comment) records, as comments are not semantically ordered

evidence.quote: CO header records contain comments and their relative order is not semantically meaningful for the alignment data.

quarantine.oracle: The alignment representation is semantically equivalent after splitting or merging adjacent CIGAR operations of the same type, as the total count per operation type is preserved

evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations of the same type (e.g., 3M5M) can be merged (8M) or split without changing the alignment semantics, as the total length per operation type is preserved.

quarantine.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads invariance
quarantine.oracle: The read semantics are preserved after stripping mate-pair-related flag bits (0x1, 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x80) when the read is not part of a paired-end library

evidence.quote: For unpaired reads, mate-pair flag bits have no meaning and should be cleared; normalizing these flags preserves the read's alignment semantics.

quarantine.oracle: The alignment semantics are preserved after toggling between hard and soft clipping representations, as both indicate clipped bases but differ only in whether the clipped bases appear in SEQ

evidence.quote: Hard clipping (H) removes bases from SEQ while soft clipping (S) retains them; both represent the same alignment with clipped bases, and converting between them preserves alignment semantics when SEQ is adjusted accordingly.

quarantine.oracle: If a SAM record is rejected due to invalid flag bit combinations, applying violate_flag_bit_exclusivity (which introduces further invalid flag combinations) should still result in rejection. The rejection status is invariant under this transform.

evidence.quote: SAM specification defines mutually exclusive flag bit combinations; records violating these are invalid.

quarantine.mr_name: Invalid optional tag type remains rejected
quarantine.oracle: If a SAM record is rejected due to an invalid optional tag type character, applying violate_optional_tag_type_character (which changes tag type characters to invalid ones) should still result in rejection. The rejection status is invariant.

evidence.quote: SAM specification defines valid tag type characters (A, i, f, Z, H, B); other characters are invalid.

quarantine.mr_name: Invalid TLEN sign consistency remains rejected
quarantine.oracle: If a SAM record is rejected due to TLEN sign inconsistency in paired reads, applying violate_tlen_sign_consistency (which flips TLEN signs) should still result in rejection. The rejection status is invariant.

evidence.quote: SAM specification requires TLEN to have opposite signs for paired reads mapping to the same chromosome.

quarantine.mr_name: Invalid CIGAR/SEQ length mismatch remains rejected
quarantine.oracle: If a SAM record is rejected due to CIGAR/SEQ length mismatch, applying violate_cigar_seq_length (which introduces further length mismatches) should still result in rejection. The rejection status is invariant.

evidence.quote: SAM specification requires that the sum of CIGAR operations consuming query bases equals the length of SEQ.

quarantine.mr_name: Position shift out of bounds remains rejected
quarantine.oracle: If a SAM record is rejected due to POS being out of bounds relative to SQ LN, applying pos_shift_with_sq_ln_bound_check (which shifts POS while checking bounds) should still result in rejection. The rejection status is invariant.

evidence.quote: SAM specification requires POS to be within the reference sequence length defined in the SQ header.
